skill.name: using-superpowers
skill.description: Guides an assistant to check for relevant skills before responding, loading instructions, or starting implementation work.
skill.summary: Requires early skill discovery and a strict workflow for using relevant assistant skills.
skill.author: obra

security_audit.summary: The static external command and weak cryptography findings are false positives caused by Markdown formatting and prose. However, the skill contains prompt-injection behavior that attempts to override tool order, response timing, and file-review behavior, so it should be blocked from publication.

critical_findings.title: Prompt Injection Attempt Detected
critical_findings.description: The skill asserts mandatory authority over assistant behavior, requires skill invocation before any response, and directs Claude Code to follow loaded skill content directly. It also tells the assistant not to read skill files, which can obstruct independent review.
critical_findings.confidence_reasoning: The text directly uses mandatory override language and attempts to control tool order before any response. The directive not to read skill files confirms an unsafe instruction hierarchy pattern.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misidentified as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The static Ruby or shell backtick finding is a false positive. Line 16 uses Markdown inline code formatting for a tool name, not executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is prose inside Markdown and contains no Ruby runtime, shell interpreter, or command invocation context.

low_findings.title: Graphviz Code Fence Misidentified as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The static Ruby or shell backtick finding is a false positive. Lines 26 through 46 are a fenced Graphviz diagram, not shell execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The detected backticks delimit a Markdown dot code block. No command is executed by the skill file itself.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Pattern False Positive
low_findings.description: The static weak cryptography findings are false positives. The cited lines contain descriptive prose about Claude Code and implementation skills, not cryptographic algorithm use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: No hash, cipher, crypto library, or encryption operation appears at the cited locations. The matches arise from ordinary words in Markdown text.

dangerous_patterns.title: Prompt Injection Attempt Detected
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill asserts mandatory authority over assistant behavior, requires skill invocation before any response, and directs Claude Code to follow loaded skill content directly. It also tells the assistant not to read skill files, which can obstruct independent review.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The text directly uses mandatory override language and attempts to control tool order before any response. The directive not to read skill files confirms an unsafe instruction hierarchy pattern.

content.user_title: Enforce Skill Use Before Assistant Work
content.value_statement: Teams need consistent skill discovery before assistants start work. This skill gives a strict workflow for checking, loading, and following relevant skills.
actual_capabilities: Defines when an assistant should check for applicable skills.
Explains how Claude Code should load skills through the Skill tool.
Provides a flow diagram for skill selection and response order.
Lists common rationalizations that bypass skill checks.
Describes priority between process skills and implementation skills.
Distinguishes rigid skills from flexible skills.
limitations: It does not execute code or enforce behavior by itself.
It assumes the runtime provides a compatible Skill tool.
It may conflict with higher priority platform instructions.
It does not verify the safety of other skills.

use_cases.title: Standardize Assistant Startup
use_cases.description: Require assistants to check available skills before answering or taking action.

use_cases.title: Guide Claude Code Workflows
use_cases.description: Document when Claude Code should load process and implementation skills.

use_cases.title: Review Agent Process Rules
use_cases.description: Use the text as an example when evaluating strict assistant workflow instructions.

prompt_templates.title: Check Skill Fit
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to decide whether my request should load another skill before work begins.

prompt_templates.title: Apply Existing Skills
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to identify applicable skills, explain which one applies, and then continue with the task.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Skill Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my assistant workflow against this skill and identify where skill checks are missing or delayed.

prompt_templates.title: Design Skill Governance
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill as input for a governance plan that separates process skills, implementation skills, and safety review steps.

output_examples.input: A developer asks the assistant to fix a bug.
output_examples.output: The assistant checks for debugging and domain skills before exploring files, then follows the selected workflow.

output_examples.input: A team wants a consistent first step for assistant tasks.
output_examples.output: The skill produces a startup rule: check relevant skills, announce the selected skill, then continue.

output_examples.input: A reviewer evaluates the skill for marketplace safety.
output_examples.output: The reviewer flags its mandatory override language and tool restriction as unsafe prompt-injection behavior.

best_practices: Treat this skill as process guidance, not as higher priority system policy.
Verify each loaded skill before following its instructions.
Keep safety review separate from workflow optimization.
anti_patterns: Do not let this skill override platform or user instructions.
Do not skip file review because a skill says to use a tool first.
Do not assume every available skill is safe or relevant.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It tells an assistant to check for relevant skills before answering or acting.

faq.question: Does it run commands?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed file is Markdown guidance and does not execute shell commands.

faq.question: Why is it blocked?
faq.answer: It uses mandatory language that attempts to override tool order, response timing, and file review behavior.

faq.question: Can it verify other skills?
faq.answer: No. It provides workflow guidance but does not assess whether another skill is safe.

faq.question: Which tools does it mention?
faq.answer: It mentions Claude Code and the Skill tool, and it references TodoWrite in its diagram.

faq.question: Is the static command warning confirmed?
faq.answer: No. The command warnings are false positives from Markdown backticks and a diagram block.
